summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike targeting Kyiv and multiple regions, reporting dozens of casualties and extensive drone activity. Ukrainian forces intercepted 642 targets, including 40 rockets and 602 UAVs, across the country. Separate incidents include Russian drone attacks on civilian structures in Donetsk and Belgorod regions.
key_points: Russia conducted a large‑scale combined airstrike with Shahed, Gerbera, and other UAVs plus rockets, focusing on Kyiv and hitting Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava and other regions, with 642 targets claimed destroyed (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Five groups of cruise missiles arrived over Kyiv region near Kagarlyk, with additional groups from Cherkasy expected (Source: @eRadarrua/94864).
Three Russian aviation bombs (FAB‑250) struck residential buildings in Druzhkivka, injuring five people (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Ukrainian air defence downed a Shahed UAV over Dimer heading toward Vyshedor (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67441).
A Russian drone hit a private house in Bochevka, Belgorod region, injuring a child (Source: @tass_agency/378544).
Ukrainian authorities reported 8 injured, including three children, after attacks in Chernihiv region, with fires in a cultural house (Source: @UkraineNow/81587).
Russia announced a ban on aviation fuel exports until 30 November, citing attacks on Russian oil facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81592).
France intercepted a sanctioned Russian oil tanker Tagor in the Atlantic Ocean (Source: @UkraineNow/81589).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike focusing on Kyiv, reporting 642 intercepted targets, while Ukrainian forces reported casualties across multiple regions. Drone and missile attacks also hit civilian infrastructure in Donetsk and Belgorod. International responses included France seizing a sanctioned Russian tanker.

points: Ukrainian air defence reported downing 602 hostile UAVs of various types, including Shahed and Gerbera, during the night of 1‑2 June, with 40 rockets also intercepted (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
The Donetsk regional prosecutor announced that three Russian aviation bombs (FAB‑250) struck multi‑apartment buildings in Druzhkivka, wounding five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
In Chernihiv region, eight people, including three children, were injured and a fire broke out in a cultural house after Russian attacks (Source: @UkraineNow/81587).
Ukrainian authorities confirmed a Shahed UAV over Dimer heading toward Vyshedor, indicating continued low‑altitude threats near Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67441).
A Russian UAV strike on a private house in Bochevka, Belgorod region, injured a child, highlighting cross‑border drone threats (Source: @tass_agency/378544).
Russia deployed cruise missile groups over Kyiv region, with five groups arriving near Kagarlyk and three more from Cherkasy expected (Source: @eRadarrua/94864).

Three Tu‑95MS bombers landed at Olenya airfield and three Tu‑160s proceeded to the Far East, later to land at the Ukrainianka airfield, indicating strategic bomber movements (Source: @eRadarrua/94896).
Russia announced a ban on aviation fuel exports until 30 November, linking the decision to Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil assets (Source: @UkraineNow/81592).
French forces seized the sanctioned Russian tanker Tagor in the Atlantic, demonstrating international enforcement of sanctions (Source: @UkraineNow/81589).
Russian authorities reported that a drone strike caused a fire at an industrial enterprise in Belgorod region (Source: @tass_agency/378549).
Shahed UAVs were reported moving from Kursk region toward Romny in the east (Source: @kpszsu/63632).
Ukrainian forces noted a group of UAVs from the east of Cherkasy heading toward Bila Tserkva (Source: @kpszsu/63630).
A UAV group from the north of Kharkiv moved toward Balakliia (Source: @kpszsu/63641).
Ukrainian defence reported UAVs from the north of Kharkiv heading directly to Kharkiv city (Source: @kpszsu/63617).
Ukrainian radar observed a Shahed UAV over Dimer heading to Vyshedor, indicating eastern approaches toward Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67441).
UAVs from northern Chernihiv region moved along the Belarus border toward Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63619).
A UAV group from northern Chernihiv headed toward northern Kyiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63624).
UAVs from northern Kyiv region were observed heading toward Kyiv itself (Source: @kpszsu/63634).
A Shahed UAV was sighted over Dimer heading toward Vyshedor, near the northern approaches to Kyiv (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67441).
Five groups of cruise missiles were reported over Kyiv region near Kagarlyk, indicating northern strike concentration (Source: @eRadarrua/94864).
UAVs from the south of Sumy region were reported heading toward Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/63646).
A Shahed UAV was observed over Dimer heading toward Vyshedor, affecting southern Kyiv suburbs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67441).
Russian aviation bombs struck Druzhkivka in Donetsk region, causing civilian injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
Ukrainian reports indicated attacks on Dnipro with six dead and 36 injured (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Attacks were also reported in Zaporizhzhia and Sumy regions during the mass strike (Source: @UkraineNow/81607).
Ukrainian air defence claimed to have downed 40 rockets and 602 UAVs during the massive night attack, including 11 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 26 X‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Five groups of cruise missiles arrived over Kyiv region near Kagarlyk, with additional groups from Cherkasy expected (Source: @eRadarrua/94864).
Three Tu‑95MS bombers landed at Olenya airfield and three Tu‑160s proceeded to the Far East, later to land at Ukrainianka airfield (Source: @eRadarrua/94896).
Russian forces used Shahed, Gerbera, and other UAV types in the combined strike across multiple oblasts (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Ukrainian radar reported multiple Shahed UAVs over Dimer heading toward Vyshedor and Vyshedor/Hostomel (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67441).

key_developments: Russia launched a massive combined airstrike with Shahed and other UAVs plus rockets, focusing on Kyiv and striking Dnipro, Kharkiv, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava and other regions, with Ukrainian forces reporting 642 intercepted targets (Source: @kpszsu/63655).

Five groups of cruise missiles arrived over Kyiv region near Kagarlyk, with three additional groups from Cherkasy expected, indicating a concentrated missile barrage on the capital (Source: @eRadarrua/94864).
Ukrainian air defence downed 602 hostile UAVs and 40 rockets, including 11 Iskander‑M ballistic missiles and 26 X‑101 cruise missiles, during the night of 1‑2 June (Source: @kpszsu/63655).
Three Russian FAB‑250 aviation bombs hit multi‑apartment buildings in Druzhkivka, Donetsk region, injuring five civilians (Source: @UkraineNow/81596).
A Russian drone strike on a private house in Bochevka, Belgorod region, injured a child, showing cross‑border UAV threats (Source: @tass_agency/378544).
France intercepted the sanctioned Russian oil tanker Tagor in the Atlantic, demonstrating enforcement of sanctions against Russia (Source: @UkraineNow/81589).
Russia announced a ban on aviation fuel exports until 30 November, citing Ukrainian attacks on Russian oil facilities (Source: @UkraineNow/81592).
sections.threat_assessment: The recent mass airstrike demonstrates Russia's capability to coordinate large‑scale UAV and missile attacks across multiple fronts, stressing Ukrainian air defence and civil infrastructure. Persistent low‑altitude Shahed UAV incursions near Kyiv and along the Belarus border indicate a sustained threat to northern approaches. Cross‑border drone activity in Belgorod highlights the expanding reach of the conflict beyond Ukraine's borders.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine reported significant civilian casualties and infrastructure damage in Donetsk, Chernihiv, Dnipro and other oblasts, prompting heightened alert levels and mobilization of air defence assets. Internationally, France's seizure of a sanctioned Russian tanker underscores continued enforcement of economic measures against Russia. Russian domestic policy changes, such as the aviation fuel export ban, reflect internal resource management amid the conflict.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths from Kursk, Cherkasy, Kharkiv and Sumy regions provides insight into Russian targeting priorities and potential staging areas. The deployment of cruise missile groups over Kyiv suggests pre‑planned strike packages that may be reused. Continued observation of cross‑border drone incidents in Belgorod is essential for assessing escalation risks beyond Ukrainian territory.
